summary_one_line: A Devanagari buffer with codepoint U+0906 immediately followed by U+093A/0945/0946/0947/0948 to enter the matching vowel-constraint case-arm.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A glyph buffer whose script is HB_SCRIPT_DEVANAGARI containing an adjacent pair: current codepoint 0x0906 (case at line 74) followed by next-glyph codepoint in {0x093A, 0x0945, 0x0946, 0x0947, 0x0948}, which makes the inner switch at line 75 take the true case-arm at line 77.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: The naive_ctx winner-resolving seed carries repeated little-endian 0x0906 / 0x0945 codepoint words near offsets 0x10-0x21 (06 09 00 00 ... 45 09 00 00), supplying both the 0x0906 head glyph and a 0x0945-family follower; on the winner the blocker line 77 records T=1 (true side taken) versus the loser's T=0.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: The naive loser-blocking seeds reach the 0x0906 case (line 74 F=28) and execute the inner switch, but the follower glyph never lands in {0x093A,0x0945,0x0946,0x0947,0x0948}, so line 77 stays at T=0 F=2 and the true branch is never taken.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: naive_ctx's CtxHook folds the call-context hash into the edge-map index, so the same vowel-constraint edge reached via the Indic preprocess_text call path becomes a fresh (call-context, edge) coverage bucket; this rewards the scheduler for keeping/mutating Devanagari inputs that drive deeper into the per-script switch, letting it hill-climb to the specific 0x0906+follower pair, whereas plain naive sees only a bare already-covered edge and gets no signal to refine the second codepoint.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the case-arm at line 77 inside the 0x0906 inner switch, with the true side globally blocked.
Reaching the blocker requires the current glyph to be 0x0906 in the Devanagari arm.
The winner-resolving seed was stored by naive_ctx and contains repeated 0x0906 and 0x0945 little-endian codepoint words.
On the winner the blocker line 77 takes the true side (T=1) while the loser stays at T=0.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L  77  T=1 F=1  T=0 F=2  case 0x093Au: case 0x0945u: case 0x0946u: case 0x0947u:  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: The loser reaches the 0x0906 case but never satisfies the follower codepoint condition.
naive_ctx's CtxHook folds the call context into the edge-map index so a (call-context, edge) pair is new coverage.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
folds it into the edge-map index, so the same basic-block edge is
recorded at different map slots depending on the call path that
reached it.

evidence_trail.claim: The decisive pair is naive_ctx > naive with delta ctx_coverage and prob_div 0.90.
naive_ctx uses no I2S substitution and no CMP_MAP.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: claimed_mechanism is context-sensitive coverage (naive_ctx CtxHook), not I2SRandReplace, so the mandatory lineage dash-row check does not apply; MECHANISM CONTEXT confirms naive_ctx has no I2SRandReplace and no CMP_MAP and emits only havoc/token mutation_op names with no dash rows.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic Devanagari harness where adding the CtxHook (naive_ctx) does NOT improve the rate of taking the 0x0906-follower true arm over plain naive at matched budget, i.e. context-indexed coverage gives no measurable advantage in resolving the nested per-codepoint switch.

weakest_evidence_point: Only one winner-resolving seed is available, so the byte-level evidence that 0x0906+0x0945 adjacency (not some other follower) is what flips line 77 is inferred from a single example rather than corroborated across seeds.
